title: Particle concentration analyzing system and method
abstract: A particle concentration analyzing system for testing particle concentrations in a fluid sample, such as engine emission particle concentration present in the exhaust of an engine. The particle concentration analyzing system includes a condensation particle counter having a saturation chamber, a condenser, and a laser optic particle counter. The analyzing system further includes a working fluid tank, a working fluid pump, and a sampling probe. The system provides a robust analysis system for a user to test vehicle emissions without being highly trained on the device, as the device is protected from misuse. A position sensitive sensor is used to ensure that the system is not damaged if the system is tipped over or placed in a position that would produce false results. Additional features include differential pressure sensors, a sealed and replaceable tamper resistant working fluid tank, a solvent recovery system, an anti-cheat device, and fluid purity sensors.
claims:
Claims The embodiments of the invention in which an exclusive property or privilege is claimed are defined as follows: 1. A particle concentration analyzing system adapted to count a particle concentration in a sample fluid, comprising: a working fluid tank adapted to contain a working fluid; a sample fluid input configured to receive the sample fluid to be analyzed; a condensation particle counter comprising a fluid saturation chamber configured to saturate an ambient air with the working fluid, a condenser configured to condense the working fluid saturated ambient air from said fluid saturation chamber onto the sample fluid, and an optical particle counter configured to count sample particles present in the sample fluid; a working fluid pump adapted to transfer the working fluid from said working fluid tank to said fluid saturation chamber; and an electronic control unit responsive to said optical particle counter to count sample particles present in the sample fluid. 2. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1 , wherein said working fluid tank, said working fluid pump, said sample fluid input, said condensation particle counter, and said electronic control unit are interconnected as an assembly and adapted to count particles in the sample fluid. 3. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1, further comprising a position sensitive switch configured to automatically disable said analyzing system when said analyzing system is not properly positioned, said position dependent switch comprises a three-axis accelerometer 4. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1 , further comprising a sampling probe for insertion into a vehicle exhaust pipe and a sampling line coupled at one end to said sampling probe and removably coupled at an opposite end to said sample fluid input upstream of said condenser, said sampling line is in fluid communication with said sample fluid input and said sampling probe. 5. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1, further comprising an exhaust pump downstream of said condensation particle counter configured to draw fluid from said condensation particle counter, said exhaust pump in electronic communication with said electronic control unit. 6. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 5, further comprising an integrated ejector diluter upstream of said condensation particle counter and a dilution pump upstream of said ejector dilutor, said ejector diluter configured to dilute the concentration of sample particles in a fluid sample at a pre-determined dilution ratio prior to the sample fluid entering said condensation particle counter, said dilution ejector diluter and said dilution pump in electronic communication with said electronic control unit. 7. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 6, further comprising a differential pressure sensor disposed along a flow path of said analyzing system configured to determine a pressure differential in said analyzing system. 8. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 7, wherein said differential pressure sensor is configured to measure a first pressure at a first pressure measuring port disposed in said system and a second pressure at a second pressure measuring port disposed in said system. 9. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 8, wherein said electronic control unit is further responsive to said exhaust pump and said dilution pump to measure either of the first pressure by selectively disabling said dilution pump and instructing said differential pressure sensor to measure said first pressure while said dilution pump is disabled and the second pressure by selectively disabling said exhaust pump and instructing said differential pressure sensor to measure said second pressure while said exhaust pump is disabled, said electronic control unit is adapted to calculate a pressure differential between said first pressure and said second pressure. 10. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1, wherein said working fluid tank is self-contained and removable from said condensation particle counter, said working fluid tank is sealed, such that it does not spill or leak when moved, flipped, or turned. 11. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 10, wherein said working fluid tank further comprises a water absorbing material for preventing contamination or deterioration of the working fluid. 12. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 11, wherein said water absorbing material is a molecular sieve disposed in said working fluid tank configured to remove contaminants from the working fluid. 13. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 11, wherein said water absorbing material is a hydroscopic material adapted to remove water contamination from the working fluid. 14. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1, further comprising an evaporator tube configured to remove semi-volatile particles from said analyzing system. 15. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1, further comprising a gas sensor disposed downstream of said working fluid tank and configured to measure purity of the working fluid transferred from said working fluid tank to said fluid saturation chamber. 16. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1, further comprising a solvent recovery system configured to recover working fluid from an exhaust flow of the sample fluid exhausted from said condensation particle counter after passing through said optical particle counter, said solvent recovery system returns recovered working fluid to said working fluid tank to be reused in said particle concentration analyzing system. 17. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 16, wherein said solvent recovery system comprises a cooling device. 18. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 4, further comprising an anti-cheat device, said anti-cheat device comprises a carbon dioxide concentration sensor adapted to measure a concentration of carbon dioxide present in the sample fluid, said anti-cheat device adapted to compare the measured carbon dioxide concentration to an expected carbon dioxide 

 concentration and verify, based on the compared concentrations, that said sampling probe is properly inserted 19. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1, wherein said working fluid pump is a peristaltic pump configured to automatically fill said working fluid tank with a working fluid. 20. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1 , further comprising a gas concentration sensor downstream of said sample fluid input and adapted to measure a gas concentration in said particle concentration analyzing system. 21. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 20, wherein said electronic control unit is further adapted to determine if said particle concentration analyzing system is properly calibrated by comparing the gas concentration measured at said gas concentration sensor to a known gas concentration of a reference gas introduced into said particle concentration analyzing system upstream of said gas concentration sensor, wherein said electronic control unit is configured to calculate and determine if the measured gas concentration is within a specified range of the known gas concentration. 22. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 21, wherein said gas concentration sensor comprises a carbon dioxide concentration sensor. 23. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1, further comprising a liquid level sensor disposed inside said fluid saturation chamber, said liquid level sensor is configured to indicate the working fluid level inside said fluid saturation chamber. 24. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1 , further comprising a fluid level float disposed inside said working fluid tank, said fluid level float configured to indicate a working fluid level inside said working fluid tank. 25. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1, wherein said electronic control unit further comprises a low-cost wireless communications system adapted to wirelessly 

 communicate with said electronic control unit to control said particle concentration analyzing system. 26. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 1 , wherein said electronic control unit further comprises a low-cost graphical user interface. 27. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 6, wherein said electronic control unit comprises a plurality of electronically controlled pump switches. 28. The particle concentration analyzing system of claim 27, wherein said plurality of electronically controlled pump switches comprise an exhaust pump switch and a working fluid pump switch, wherein said exhaust pump switch is configured to electronically control the power to the exhaust pump, and said working fluid pump switch is configured to electronically control the power to the working fluid pump. 29. A particle concentration analyzing system adapted to count a particle concentration in a sample fluid, comprising: a working fluid tank adapted to contain a working fluid; a sample fluid input configured to receive the sample fluid to be analyzed; a condensation particle counter comprising a fluid saturation chamber configured to saturate an ambient air with the working fluid, a condenser configured to condense the working fluid saturated ambient air from said fluid saturation chamber onto the sample fluid, and an optical particle counter configured to count sample particles present in the sample fluid; a working fluid pump adapted to transfer the working fluid from said working fluid tank to said fluid saturation chamber; a position sensitive switch configured to automatically disable said analyzing system when said analyzing system is not properly positioned, said position dependent switch comprises a three-axis accelerometer; an exhaust pump downstream of said condensation particle counter; an integrated ejector diluter upstream of said condensation particle counter and a dilution pump upstream of said ejector dilutor, said ejector diluter configured to dilute the concentration 

 of sample particles in a fluid sample at a pre-determined dilution ratio prior to the sample fluid entering said condensation particle counter; a differential pressure sensor disposed along a flow path of said analyzing system configured to determine a pressure differential in said analyzing system; an electronic control unit responsive to said exhaust pump, said dilution pump, and said differential pressure sensor to determine a pressure differential between said exhaust pump and said dilution; wherein said electronic control unit is further responsive to selectively disable said dilution pump and to instruct said differential pressure sensor to measure an exhaust pump pressure while said dilution pump is disabled and said exhaust pump is operating and to selectively disable said exhaust pump and to instmct said differential pressure sensor to measure a dilution pump pressure while said exhaust pump is disabled and said dilution pump is operating, said controller is adapted to calculate a pressure differential between the exhaust pump pressure and the dilution pump pressure based on the pressures measured at the differential pressure sensor. 30. A particle concentration analyzing system adapted to count a particle concentration in a sample fluid, comprising: a sample fluid input configured to receive the sample fluid to be analyzed; a condensation particle counter comprising a fluid saturation chamber configured to saturate an ambient air with a working fluid, a condenser configured to condense the working fluid saturated ambient air from said fluid saturation chamber onto the sample fluid, and an optical particle counter configured to count sample particles present in the sample fluid; and a position sensitive switch configured to automatically disable said analyzing system when said analyzing system is not properly positioned, said position dependent switch comprises a three-axis accelerometer. 31. A particle concentration analyzing system adapted to count a particle concentration in a sample fluid, comprising: a condensation particle counter comprising a fluid saturation chamber configured to saturate an ambient air with a working fluid, a condenser configured to condense the working 

 fluid saturated ambient air from said fluid saturation chamber onto the sample fluid, and an optical particle counter configured to count sample particles present in the sample fluid; a sample fluid input upstream of said condensation particle counter configured to receive the sample fluid to be analyzed; and a solvent recovery system comprising a cooling device, said solvent recovery system configured to recover working fluid from an exhaust flow of the sample fluid that is exhausted from said condensation particle counter after passing through said optical particle counter; wherein said solvent recovery system returns recovered working fluid to said a fluid saturation chamber to be reused in said particle concentration analyzing system. 32. A particle concentration analyzing system adapted to count a particle concentration in a sample fluid, comprising: a sample fluid input configured to receive the sample fluid to be analyzed; a condensation particle counter comprising a fluid saturation chamber configured to saturate an ambient air with a working fluid, a condenser configured to condense the working fluid saturated ambient air from said fluid saturation chamber onto the sample fluid, and an optical particle counter configured to count sample particles present in the sample fluid; and an anti-cheat device adapted to measure a concentration of a gas present in the sample fluid, said anti-cheat device adapted to compare the measured gas concentration to an expected gas concentration and to verify, based on the compared concentrations, that said particle concentration analyzing system is properly configured. 33. A particle concentration analyzing system adapted to count a particle concentration in a sample fluid, comprising: a sample fluid input configured to receive the sample fluid to be analyzed; a condensation particle counter comprising a fluid saturation chamber configured to saturate an ambient air with a working fluid, a condenser configured to condense the working fluid saturated ambient air from said fluid saturation chamber onto the sample fluid, and an optical particle counter configured to count sample particles present in the sample fluid; an electronic control unit responsive to said optical particle counter to count sample particles present in the sample fluid; and 

 a gas concentration sensor downstream of said condensation particle counter adapted to measure a gas concentration in said particle concentration analyzing system; wherein said electronic control unit is further responsive to said gas concentration sensor to determine if said particle concentration analyzing system is properly calibrated by comparing the gas concentration measured at said gas concentration sensor to a known gas concentration of a reference gas introduced into said particle concentration analyzing system upstream of said condensation particle counter, wherein said electronic control unit is configured to calculate and determine if the measured gas concentration is within a required range of the known gas concentration. 34. A particle concentration analyzing system adapted to count a particle concentration in a sample fluid, comprising: a working fluid tank adapted to contain a working fluid; a sample fluid input configured to receive the sample fluid to be analyzed; a condensation particle counter comprising a fluid saturation chamber configured to saturate an ambient air with the working fluid, a condenser configured to condense the working fluid saturated ambient air from said fluid saturation chamber onto the sample fluid, and an optical particle counter configured to count sample particles present in the sample fluid; a working fluid pump adapted to transfer the working fluid from said working fluid tank to said fluid saturation chamber; an electronic control unit responsive to said optical particle counter to count sample particles present in the sample fluid; and a gas concentration sensor disposed downstream of said working fluid tank and configured to measure purity of the working fluid transferred from said working fluid tank to said fluid saturation chamber; wherein said electronic control unit is further responsive to said gas concentration sensor to disable said particle concentration analyzing system when the purity of the working fluid is below a required level of purity. 35. A method for analyzing particle concentrations in a fluid sample, said method comprising: 

 directing a sample of sample fluid to a particle concentration analyzing system; pumping a working fluid from a working fluid tank to a fluid saturator block with a working fluid pump; heating the working fluid in said fluid saturator block; directing a flow of ambient air into said fluid saturator block; saturating the ambient air with the heated working fluid to produce a super-saturated fluid flow; transporting the super-saturated fluid to a mixing chamber: mixing the super-saturated fluid with the sample of sample fluid in said mixing chamber to create a mixture of the sample fluid and the super-saturated fluid; transporting the mixture into a condenser downstream of said mixing chamber; condensing the super-saturated fluid onto sample particles in the sample fluid to grow the sample particles; transporting the grown sample particles through an optical particle counter; counting the grown sample particles with said optical particle counter; receiving information from said optical particle counter at an electronic control unit in electronic communication with said optical particle counter; determining a concentration of the sample particles of the sample fluid with said electronic control unit; and exhausting the counted sample particles from said optical particle counter. 36. The method of claim 35, further comprising diluting the sample fluid in an ejector dilutor and transporting the diluted sample to said mixing chamber by a dilution pump. 37. The method of claim 36, further comprising alternatingly disabling said dilution pump and said exhaust pump with said electronic control unit, said electronic control unit comprising a plurality of pump switches, measuring pressures between said dilution pump and said exhaust pump with a differential pressure sensor in electronic communication with said electronic control unit while alternatingly disabling said dilution and exhaust pumps, and calculating, with said electronic control unit, a pressure differential between said dilution pump and said exhaust pump. 38. The method of claim 35, further comprising monitoring a fluid level in said fluid saturator chamber with a saturator fluid level sensor, monitoring a fluid level in said working fluid tank with a fluid tank level sensor, communicating information of the measured fluid levels to said electronic control unit, and automatically refilling said working fluid in said fluid saturator chamber based on the information received by the electronic controller from the saturator fluid level sensor and the fluid tank level sensor. 39. The method of claim 35, further comprising disabling said particle concentration analyzing system with a position sensitive safety switch if said particle concentration analyzing system is not in an ideal operating position, said position sensitive safety switch comprises a three-axis accelerometer, said position sensitive safety switch configured to disable said particle concentration analyzing system if said three-axis accelerometer detects that said particle concentration analyzing system is improperly positioned. 40. The method of claim 35, further comprising recovering working fluid from the exhausted counted aerosol with a solvent recovery system and returning the recovered working fluid to said working fluid tank to be recycled and reused in said particle concentration analyzing system. 41. The method of claim 35, further comprising measuring a concentration of carbon dioxide present in the sample fluid with a gas concentration sensor that is in electronic communication with said electronic control unit and verifying that a sampling probe is properly inserted into a vehicle exhaust pipe with said electronic control unit by comparing a measured gas concentration from said gas concentration sensor with an expected exhaust gas concentration. 42. The method of claim 35, further comprising introducing a reference fluid flow into said particle concentration analyzing system at a reference gas inlet port upstream of said condenser, said reference fluid having a known particle concentration, measuring a particle concentration with a gas sensor disposed at a location in said particle concentration analyzing system downstream of said condenser, said gas sensor is in electronic communication with said electronic control unit, receiving the measured particle concentration from said gas sensor at said electronic control unit, comparing, with said electronic control unit, the measured particle concentration to an expected particle concentration based on the known particle concentration of 

 the reference fluid, and verifying that said particle concentration analyzing system flow values are within required calibration margins based on the comparison of measured particle concentration and expected particle concentration. 43. The method of claim 35, further comprising measuring the purity of the working fluid from the working fluid tank with a gas purity sensor, said gas purity sensor is in electronic communication with said electronic control unit, and verifying, with said electronic control unit, that said working fluid meets a required purity. 44. A method for analyzing particle concentrations in a fluid sample, said method comprising: introducing a reference fluid flow into said particle concentration analyzing system at a reference gas inlet port upstream of said condenser, said reference fluid having a known particle concentration; measuring a particle concentration with a gas sensor in electronic communication with said electronic control unit, said gas sensor disposed at a location in said particle concentration analyzing system downstream of said condenser; receiving information of the measured particle concentration from said gas sensor at said electronic control unit; comparing, with said electronic control unit, the measured particle concentration to an expected particle concentration based on the known particle concentration of the reference fluid; and verifying that said particle concentration analyzing system flow values are calibrated within required calibration margins based on the comparison of measured particle concentration and expected particle concentration. 45. A method for analyzing particle concentrations in a fluid sample, said method comprising: monitoring a fluid level in a fluid saturator chamber of said particle concentration analysis system with a saturator fluid level sensor; 

 monitoring a fluid level in a working fluid tank disposed with said particle concentration analysis system with a fluid tank level sensor; communicating information of the measured fluid levels to an electronic control unit of said particle concentration analysis system, and pumping a working fluid with a working fluid pump from said working fluid tank to a working fluid reservoir disposed in said fluid saturator chamber based on the information received by the electronic control unit from said saturator fluid level sensor and said fluid tank level sensor. 46. A method for analyzing particle concentrations in a fluid sample, said method comprising: determining, with a pressure sensitive position sensor, if said particle concentration analysis system is properly positioned; and automatically disabling said particle concentration analysis system if said pressure sensitive position sensor determines that said particle concentration analysis system is not properly positioned. 47. A method for analyzing particle concentrations in a fluid sample, said method comprising: alternatingly disabling a dilution pump and an exhaust pump with an electronic control unit, said electronic control unit comprising a plurality of pump switches, measuring pressures between said dilution pump and said exhaust pump with said differential pressure sensor in electronic communication with said electronic control unit while said electronic control unit is alternatingly disabling said dilution and exhaust pumps; and calculating, with said electronic control unit, a pressure differential between said dilution pump and said exhaust pump based on measured pressures between said dilution pump and said exhaust pump.
